subject: Ecology
title: Rafting
description: A quick, practical guide to Rafting.

hook.text: After a flood, tangled mats of vegetation drift out to sea—carrying living passengers.

buildup.text: Lizards, insects, and small mammals have survived weeks on floating debris to reach islands.

discovery.text: DNA evidence confirms that iguanas rafted from South America to the Galápagos on storm debris.

twist.text: Rafting is rare for any one individual, but over millions of years it happens reliably.

climax.text: Madagascar's entire lemur lineage likely arrived from Africa on a single ancient raft event.

punchline.text: An accidental voyage can populate a continent.

quiz.question: How did iguanas likely reach the Galápagos?
options: By rafting on storm debris from South America
By walking on a land bridge
